title: Podman unable to resolve DNS queries(?) — aka pulling images does not work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # **kind bug**

**Description**

Pulling new images does not work. Podman returns `no such host` for all of the registries it tries to hit. This issue does not happen with Docker on the same system.

I also tried running with SELinux set to permissive, since I had similar issues [once before](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1585802) with Docker. However, there was no change with SELinux set to permissive.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run --rm -it fedora bash

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora:latest...Failed
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...Failed
Trying to pull quay.io/fedora:latest...Failed
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest...Failed
Trying to pull registry.centos.org/fedora:latest...Failed
unable to pull fedora: 5 errors occurred:

* Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://fedora:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io: no such host
* Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: no such host
* Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://quay.io/fedora:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://quay.io/v2/: dial tcp: lookup quay.io: no such host
* Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/fedora:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.access.redhat.com: no such host
* Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.centos.org/fedora:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.centos.org/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.centos.org: no such host
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
[root@hostname /] # 
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

I'm using the official Fedora package.

```
podman version 0.10.1.3
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.10.1.3-1.gitdb08685.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 4a03e555ee4105308fa4d814d4ce02a059af0b7f-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "28"
  MemFree: 10145501184
  MemTotal: 16693481472
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-59.dev.gitccb5efd.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 8388603904
  SwapTotal: 8388603904
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: fossbook.justinwflory.com
  kernel: 4.19.5-200.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 3h 57m 47.64s (Approximately 0.12 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Bare-metal Fedora 28 Workstation (4.19.5-200.fc28.x86_64).

comments.body:
What are the contents of `/etc/resolve.conf`?
Can you pull images with other Go programs (`buildah`, `skopeo`)?

Do you have an `/etc/containers/registries.conf` ?  And if so, what is inside it. Also, does podman --log-level=debug reveal anything interesting? also, mind updating to the latest podman version? you are reporting against an old version.
@baude That is the latest on F28 for now, until we can get 0.12.1 out the door tomorrow. Assuming CI doesn't decide to take a vacation again

> **@mheon**:
> What are the contents of /etc/resolve.conf?

It looks like this:

```
# Generated by NetworkManager
search justinwflory.com
nameserver 192.168.1.1
```

For debugging, I added `nameserver 1.1.1.1`, but nothing changed.

> **@baude**:
> Do you have an /etc/containers/registries.conf ? And if so, what is inside it.

Yes, see below.

```
[registries.search]
registries = ['docker.io', 'registry.fedoraproject.org', 'quay.io', 'registry.access.redhat.com', 'registry.centos.org']

[registries.insecure]
registries = []

[registries.block]
registries = []
```

> **@baude**:
> Also, does podman --log-level=debug reveal anything interesting?

I'm not sure if this is helpful or not. I stopped copying after the first host failed for readability's sake, since the rest were duplicates of this error:

```

DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest" 
DEBU[0000] reference "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest" does not resolve to an image ID 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest" 
DEBU[0000] reference "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest" does not resolve to an image ID

DEBU[0000] reference "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]localhost/ubuntu:latest" does not resolve to an image ID 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest" 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/jflory/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]registry.fedoraproject.org/ubuntu:latest"

DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v1/_ping

DEBU[0000] Error pulling image ref //ubuntu:latest: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://ubuntu:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io: no such host

```

> **@baude**:
> also, mind updating to the latest podman version? you are reporting against an old version.

Once the 0.12.1.1 package update in [Bodhi](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2018-e8e19475eb) pushes to the testing repos for F28, I can re-test.

> **_@mheon_**:
> @jwflory Anything unusual about your setup?

I can't guess what. I don't work with Golang often so I haven't made many changes. The only thing I've consciously changed about my Golang environment is setting a custom `$GOPATH` somewhere else in my user's home directory.

I also had a similar issue in Fedora 27 with Docker ([bug 1585802](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1585802)). However, the problem there was SELinux. Reinstalling some packages let Docker resolve DNS again. I did set SELinux to permissive with Podman when debugging for this ticket, but nothing changed then either.

Does pulling a fully qualified image work?
i.e.

```
podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29
```
@jwflory is this still a problem?
@baude I haven't had a chance to run this again on the affected machine. I'll try to update soon and try again ASAP. Sorry for the slow response, I am taking semester exams this week.

Thanks for your patience. I was able to re-test now.

> @baude:
> also, mind updating to the latest podman version? you are reporting against an old version.

I updated to 0.12.1.1 and this bug is still present.

> @baude:
> Does pulling a fully qualified image work?

Unfortunately, still no dice:

```
$ podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29

Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29...Failed
error pulling image "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29": unable to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: no such host
```
this has me baffled.  is that same user able to resolve names to IP addresses normally?
@baude In a non-container context? I'm able to successfully resolve DNS with `host` in the command line.

You are running podman inside of a container?  Or directly on the host?  
Are you doing this with non-root podman?
Does sudo podman pull 
Work for you ?

> You are running podman inside of a container? Or directly on the host?

I am running it directly on my host.

>  Are you doing this with non-root podman?

Yes, I am running as my user.

> Does `sudo podman pull` work for you ?

Yes, actually this worked perfectly:

```
$ sudo podman pull fedora
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:cd6c8343b59020c6bef022d3f81902a428b0552f1b3c0938fea36383eb68ff6c
 85.64 MB / 85.64 MB [=====================================================] 10s
Copying config sha256:25e6809f6fabf364c645430961bb3a02adc5b93f7bcdc8a41b692100cd0aa8bd
 2.20 KB / 2.20 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
25e6809f6fabf364c645430961bb3a02adc5b93f7bcdc8a41b692100cd0aa8bd
```
And podman pull fails when you run as non root.

> And podman pull fails when you run as non root.

Yes, it fails.

jwflory could you attempt to wget off of that host?

If you pull an image from docker.io does it work?

> jwflory could you attempt to wget off of that host?

`wget` works as expected on my host with my user.

> If you pull an image from docker.io does it work?

Yes, I am able to pull an image from docker.io with `docker` CLI as my user without root (I am in the `docker` group). A similar issue was once reported with Docker in [bug 1585802](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1585802).

> To isolate this a bit more, please create a foo.go and check whether go run foo.go fails/succeeds in the same way as podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest

Seems like it worked? I can't tell. It seemed to resolve the DNS successfully (thus `proxy.fedoraproject.org`, which I thought would be different from outright failure to look-up the hostname.

```
$ go run foo.go 
Error: <nil>
Body:
===
HTTP/2.0 200 OK
Content-Length: 2
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Age: 29
Appserver: proxy09.fedoraproject.org
Apptime: D=1062
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Date: Thu, 03 Jan 2019 21:03:06 GMT
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Referrer-Policy: same-origin
Server: Apache/2.4.34 (Fedora)
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Vary: Accept
Via: 1.1 varnish (Varnish/5.1)
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
X-Varnish: 16105005 16985940
X-Xss-Protection: 1; mode=block

{}
===
```

> Seems like it worked? I can't tell

Yes.  That’s even more surprising. I guess it’s one of:

- We are doing something to affect the DNS lookup inside the code? I can’t see what that would be.
- There is a difference between the `go` environments compiling the test program and `podman` (different versions, CGO disabled/enabled, …)
- There is something specific about the `podman` binary on the target system (different SELinux context)

IIRC you said that `podman pull` breaks but `sudo podman pull` works, so tracking down where that behavior diverges (perhaps via strike) could be a next step forward.

> There is something specific about the `podman` binary on the target system (different SELinux context)

The current context on `/usr/bin/podman` is: `system_u:object_r:container_runtime_exec_t:s0`

> IIRC you said that `podman pull` breaks but `sudo podman pull` works, so tracking down where that behavior diverges (perhaps via strike) could be a next step forward.

Let me know if I can do anything to help test further.

Maybe an strace would help, could you attach that.  
Are you able to get this to happen on other machines?  If you create a new clean account does it work.

sudu sh
useradd test1
su - test1
podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29

> Maybe an strace would help, could you attach that.

This is my first time using `strace` for debugging like this. What's the best way for me to use it that is most helpful for you all?

> Are you able to get this to happen on other machines? If you create a new clean account does it work.

On another machine originally installed with Fedora 28, I do not have this issue. This laptop was originally a F23/F24 install. It's on my list to eventually do a fresh F29 install sometime in February when I have time.

Looks like the same problem with the clean account though:

```
[root@fossbook ~]# useradd test-podman
[root@fossbook ~]# su - test-podman 
[test-podman@fossbook ~]$ podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29...Failed
error pulling image "registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29": unable to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29: unable to pull image: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: no such host
```

If you do `buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29`, does that succeed?

It seems like name resolution is getting screwed up for a single command?

> If you do buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29, does that succeed?

This fails too:

```
$ buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29
Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:29: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/: dial tcp: lookup registry.fedoraproject.org: no such host
ERRO[0000] exit status 1
```

This has been fixed in buildah. We need similar fixes in podman.

@baude Could you take a look at the fixes in buildah.
Oh awesome, glad to hear this was fixed in `buildah`. Just curious… what did the problem end up being? It's been hard for me to reproduce in any environment except the one where I face this bug.
@jwflory We had to do a major rewrite/simplification of buildha pull.  There were many issues in parsing that had leaked all through the code.  We cleaned them all up.
Sadly no one has worked on this.  @mtrmac Do you have any time to work on this to clean it up.

@rhatdan If you know this has been fixed in `Buildah`, please point out the fixes.

I have no idea what is going on, and apart from randomly diffing `strace`, I have no other way to move forward without a reproducer I can try locally.
@mtrmac I have no idea, All I know is we did a major rework on buildah pull and would like to see similar done to podman pull.  For the specific Issue, I have no idea if this is fixed in buildah yet.
For what it's worth, this issue was resolved on my "problem" machine. I can pull images and I haven't done a fresh install yet either.
Could have been some network setup problems on this machine?

Here is maybe some help for anyone who runs into this issue. In my situation I was running podman on Debian Bookworm in a (vagrant managed) Virtualbox VM. I had exactly the issue as the reporter of the issue (`podman pull` not working with error `dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io on 10.0.2.3:53: no such host`, but DNS lookups, e.g. via `host` or `nslookup` worked fine). 

I solved the issue by enabling the *Host's Resolver as a DNS Proxy in NAT Mode* using `vboxmanage modifyvm "debian" --natdnshostresolver1 on`, or in the `Vagrantfile` with 

```ruby
config.vm.provider "virtualbox" do |v|
  ... # other configuration

  v.customize [
    "modifyvm",
    :id,
    "--natdnshostresolver1",
    "on"
  ]
end
```

References:
I found the solution here: https://serverfault.com/questions/453185/vagrant-virtualbox-dns-10-0-2-3-not-working
some more details about the DNS resovler configuration options https://www.virtualbox.org/manual/ch09.html#nat-adv-dns
